bags.notes: Ripe peach, white florals, fresh blueberries
Peach Tea, Sugarcane, Stone Fruit, Floral

brews.pourTechnique: 60g@0:00 / 42g@0:45 / 51g@1:30 / 51g@2:10 / 51g@2:45; gentle center bloom, slow spirals, no hard agitation
brews.notes: Excellent cup. Low acidity, no harshness. Slow drawdown gave sweet, balanced result.

brews.pourTechnique: 66g@0:00 / 36g@0:45 / 51g@1:30 / 51g@2:10 / 51g@2:45; gentle center bloom, slow spirals, no hard agitation
brews.notes: Heavier first pour did NOT raise sweetness (flat at 3.5 vs brew 1). Extraction up overall. Confounds: filter changed, bean ~1 day older. Likely 3.5 = this washed Tabi's sweetness ceiling.

brews.pourTechnique: 45g bloom + gentle stir, 45s; fill to 240g + 1 gentle stir; steep to 3:30 (switch closed/UP); flip open (DOWN), gentle swirl, drawdown
brews.notes: Enjoyable, clean. Big fragrance, very low bitterness, sweet; acidity moderate. Body very light/slightly thin — Body=5 (lightest) reads as a MILD NEGATIVE here; wanted more body. Grind 7.5 was a slip (target 8.0) but fine in immersion. Cooling lifted lychee/rose. 22 days off roast.
brews.learnings: 86C likely caused BOTH the thinness and only-moderate acidity (low temp extracts acids over sugars/body). Next: raise to 88-90C (keep grind 7.5, 1:16) to build body + sweetness and round acidity; if still thin, bump dose to 16g (1:15). One lever at a time.

brews.pourTechnique: Bloom 48g (gentle swirl @0:10, rest to 0:45); concentric pours toward 102/154/205/256g targets. 3rd pour OVERSHOT to 170g; 4th pour kept the planned increment so total ran to 270g (1:16.9). Drawdown stalled — WDT stir after the final pour to break the clog and complete drawdown.
brews.notes: V60 on a natural sundried. Grind 6.9 (finer than the 7.0 floor) + the natural's fines packed the bed -> 6:00 drawdown (target ~3:30) -> over-extracted. 3rd pour overshot to 170g; total 270g. Cup: intense Flavour & long Aftertaste, Body came up heavy (got the body), but Balance & Fragrance pulled in and Sweetness only mid. Classic mild over-extraction. Sensory scores read from the notebook radar - confirm/adjust.
brews.learnings: Body gain was a side effect of over-extraction, not a clean win - it cost Balance/Fragrance/Sweetness. For a natural on V60, 6.9 is too fine (fines clog -> long drawdown): go 7.0-7.2 and hit 256g exactly to land ~3:30. To build body cleanly, change brewer (Switch / flat-bottom) or lower the ratio - not finer grind. One lever at a time.

brews.pourTechnique: Bloom 48g (gentle swirl @0:10, rest to 0:45); pours hit targets cleanly: 102g @0:45 / 154g @1:30 / 205g @2:10 / 256g @2:45. Ratio landed exactly 1:16. Drawdown stalled near the end so WDT stir after the final pour to break the clog and move flow.
brews.notes: Executed the corrected V60 recipe (Brew Ideas row added 5/27): 7.2 grind, 16:256 hit exactly, 92°C, clean 4:6 pours. Bed still clogged at the final pour — WDT stir to break it; even with stir, total 5:30 (target 3:00–3:30). Cup clearly better than 5/27: drinker called it 'punchier'. Scores per user's framework (0.5 intervals; Body 5 = enjoyable-light, lower = thicker; Low Acidity / Low Bitterness: 5 = low).
brews.learnings: 7.2 + clean pours fixed the over-extraction without losing body — confirms the 5/27 diagnosis. BUT even at 7.2 the natural clogs V60; a WDT stir after the final pour is the de facto technique for Mpemba on V60. To eliminate the stir, try 7.4–7.5 next. Fragrance moderate (3) — possibly the ~36-day rest; Body landed close to enjoyable-light (4) on the user's framework.

brews.pourTechnique: All 350g in fast at 0:00; gentle stir @0:30; break crust + skim foam/floaters at 4:00; plunger rested on top, no press; decant carefully off the top ~5:00-5:30
brews.notes: Constraint brew: pre-ground at 7.0 (medium-fine, finer than ideal for FP) and no paper filter on hand. Stopped at ~5:30 vs planned 9:00. Cup very weak across fragrance/sweetness/flavour, but bitterness moderate (3) — unbalanced/aromatic-starved extraction, NOT pure under-extraction. 93C too low for Sey's very-light washed development; aromatic compounds didn't volatilize while bitter compounds still came through.
brews.learnings: For Sey washed light roasts, anchor temp at near-boil regardless of brewer — don't drop temp to compensate for finer grind. Diagnostic signature: low fragrance + low sweetness + bitterness still present + not sour = aromatic-starved extraction from insufficient heat. Saved as feedback memory ('Sey light roasts want near-boil').

ideas.name: Sey x Fellow - Washed Light 'House' Method
ideas.bestFor: Sey washed light roasts: Marlene Rojas (Gesha), Sey Ruarai AA (Kenya); also Counter Culture Apollo (washed Ethiopian)
ideas.steps:
Rinse filter. Bloom to 60g, wait 40s. Continuous pours: to 160g (swirl when high), to 260g, to 360g (swirl 1-2x).
Why: Roaster's own style for light washed coffees. High ratio + boiling water push sweetness/clarity and fight sour under-extraction.

ideas.name: Tetsu Kasuya 4:6 (sweet-leaning split)
ideas.bestFor: Delicate/floral & gesha: Marlene Rojas; clean washed Ethiopians (Apollo); Onyx Tropical Weather
ideas.temp: ~92-93C standard; push to ~95C for very light Sey
ideas.steps:
First 40% in 2 pours (smaller-first/larger-second = sweeter); last 60% in 3 pours (cleaner). e.g. 40 -> 100 / 150 / 200 / 250.
Why: Designed by Kasuya on a Panama Gesha. Crisp, high clarity; sweetness/acidity tunable via the first two pours.

ideas.name: Hoffmann 'Better 1-Cup' V60
ideas.bestFor: Any single-cup washed/light when you want balanced clarity: Marlene Rojas, Apollo, Ruarai
ideas.steps:
Bloom 50g; swirl at 0:10. Pour to 100g (0:45), 150g (~1:10), 200g (~1:30), 250g (~1:50); swirl at 2:00; let drain.
Why: Two-stage pours + swirls = even, forgiving, clean extraction. Reliable default for a single cup.

ideas.name: Hario Switch - Full Immersion (Clever-style)
ideas.bestFor: Forgiving sweetness/body; naturals & anaerobics: Salawara CM Natural, Genetics Blossom Naturals; or any cup tasting thin/sour
ideas.steps:
Switch UP (closed). Add all water, gentle stir to saturate. Steep 4 min. Swirl flat, flip switch open, drawdown ~45s.
Why: Immersion = even extraction, big grind leeway, accentuates sweetness & body, low astringency risk.

ideas.name: Hario Switch - Pour-over -> Immersion Hybrid
ideas.bestFor: High-acidity coffees you want rounder & sweeter: Sey Ruarai AA Kenya; bright naturals
ideas.temp: Percolation hot (~95C); then add ~20g room-temp water to bring immersion to ~82C
ideas.steps:
Switch DOWN (open): bloom 30g, pour to ~130g circular. Switch UP: add 90g + 20g cool water; steep 1-2 min. Flip open; swirl.
Why: Percolation phase = bright top notes/clarity; cooler immersion phase = sweetness/body without extra astringency.

ideas.name: Lance Hedrick high-agitation light-roast (1-2-1 style)
ideas.bestFor: Very light/dense roasts that brew sour or muted: Sey light roasts (Marlene Rojas, Ruarai) when under-extracting
ideas.steps:
Make a divot; bloom ~54g (3x) with vigorous swirl/agitation; pour rest from height with agitation; finish gentle small circles. Faster than 2:30 -> finer; slower than 3:30 -> coarser.
Why: Maximizes extraction on stubborn light roasts; heat + agitation fight sourness. CAUTION: agitation can add astringency - use only if a clean light roast still tastes sour/weak.

ideas.name: Marlene Rojas - Flat-bottom (your recommended baseline)
ideas.bestFor: Sey Marlene Rojas El Nevado, washed Gesha, Peru
ideas.steps:
Bloom 40g/45s; sweet-leaning 4:6 -> 100g (0:45) / 152g (1:30) / 204g (2:15) / 256g (2:50), gentle swirl. No hard agitation.
Why: Even extraction + roaster-aligned. Targets sweetness & low harsh acidity - your stated profile.
ideas.source: Derived (Sey/Fellow + Tetsu 4:6 + your validated flat-bottom brews)

ideas.name: Marlene Rojas - V60 (florals chase, after baseline)
ideas.bestFor: Marlene Rojas Gesha — once you have a baseline
ideas.steps:
4:6 scaled: bloom 40g/45s, then to 100 / 150 / 200 / 250. Gentle pours.
Why: Max clarity & floral lift; brighter, lighter body, less forgiving than flat-bottom.

ideas.name: Ratnagiri Hydro Honey — V60 (clarity & florals)
ideas.bestFor: Rossette Ratnagiri Hydro Honey — Sequential Fermented Hydro Honey, SLN 9, light roast, Chikmagalur (India). Roasted 2026-04-28 (~28 days off roast at first brew). Also fits other light Indian honey/fermented lots.
ideas.grind: 6.8 to start (a hair finer than your 7.0 V60 default — 28 days off roast wants more extraction). If it turns drying/muddy, go back to 7.0–7.2.
ideas.temp: Full boil (~96°C at your altitude). Heat lifts the peach-tea/floral aromatics and fights sour under-extraction on a rested light roast.
ideas.steps:
Rinse filter, preheat. 4:6, clarity-leaning. Bloom to 45g, gentle swirl at 0:10, rest to 0:45. Then 4 even pours, slow concentric circles (NO hard agitation): to 100g (start 0:45), to 150g (~1:20), to 200g (~1:50), to 250g (~2:20). Tiny swirl at ~2:25 to flatten the bed; let drain.
Why: V60 = maximum clarity and floral/peach-tea lift with a light, tea-like body — the top-note profile you asked for. Boiling water + slightly finer grind counter the sour/muted risk at 28 days off roast; the 4:6 split keeps it clean and lets you tune brightness via the first two pours (bigger first pour = brighter, smaller = sweeter).
ideas.source: Derived (your validated Tetsu 4:6 + Hoffmann V60 structure + Rossette tasting notes; tuned for the 28-day rest).

ideas.name: Mpemba V60 — corrected (fixes the 6-min drawdown)
ideas.bestFor: Counter Culture Mpemba (Burundi natural sundried; fig/strawberry/creamy). Also any natural that brews too slow / over-extracted on V60.
ideas.grind: 7.2 to start — COARSER than the 6.9 that clogged on 5/27 (naturals throw fines that pack the bed). If it comes out sour/hollow, step to 7.0. Do NOT go finer than 7.0 on this natural.
ideas.steps:
Tetsu 4:6, sweet-leaning, gentle concentric pours. Bloom 48g (swirl @0:10, rest to 0:45); to 102g (0:45); to 154g (1:30); to 205g (2:10); to 256g (2:45). STOP at 256 — hit targets exactly. EXPECT the bed to clog around the final pour (Mpemba's natural fines do this — observed 5/27 & 5/28): a gentle WDT stir after the last pour to break the clog and move drawdown is the de facto technique here. If you'd rather skip the stir, try 7.4–7.5 grind next.
Why: Direct fix to 5/27 (which ran 6:00 and over-extracted). Coarser grind + exact pours recovered Sweetness, Balance and Bitterness that the over-extraction flattened, while holding Body. V60 body stays moderate — accept that here; this recipe chases a clean, balanced cup, not body. WDT stir documents what was actually needed, not the ideal.
ideas.targetTime: 5:30 with WDT stir (observed 5/28). 3:00–3:30 target likely needs 7.4–7.5 grind.
ideas.source: Derived from your 2026-05-27 Mpemba V60 brew + diagnostics
ideas.result: Cup clearly improved vs 5/27. Today's scores per user's rating framework (0.5 intervals; Body 5 = enjoyable-light, lower = thicker; Low Acidity / Low Bitterness: 5 = low): Sweetness 3.5, Flavour 4, Balance 4, Aftertaste 3.5, Body 4, Low Acidity 3.5, Low Bitterness 4.5, Fragrance 3. Drinker called it 'punchier'. Drawdown 5:30 with WDT stir after the final pour (bed clogs even at 7.2). Comparison with 5/27 deltas is PENDING — 5/27 was scored before the framework was made explicit and needs re-reading. Next nudge: try 7.4–7.5 to see if the WDT stir becomes unnecessary.

ideas.name: Mpemba — Hario Switch immersion (for body / mouthfeel)
ideas.bestFor: Counter Culture Mpemba & other naturals when you want BODY without the over-extraction harshness the V60 gave on 5/27.
ideas.grind: 8.0–8.5 (coarser; immersion has big grind leeway and won't clog the way the V60 did).
ideas.temp: 90–91°C — natural, so cooler than washed, but NOT as low as the 86°C that left Genetics Blossom Naturals thin.
ideas.steps:
Switch UP (closed). Add all water, gentle stir to saturate. Steep ~3:30–4:00. Flip switch DOWN (open), gentle swirl, drawdown ~45s.
Why: The 5/27 lesson: on the V60 the body you got came from over-extraction, which cost Balance & Fragrance. Immersion builds body the clean way — even extraction, low astringency — so you get the mouthfeel you wanted without dragging out harsh solids. This is the right lever for body, not grinding a V60 finer.
ideas.source: Derived (your Switch immersion idea + 5/27 Mpemba learnings + Blossom Naturals temp lesson)

recipes.name: Ultimate V60 technique
recipes.steps: Bloom 60g/45s; pour to 150g by 1:15; to 250g by 1:45; gentle swirl; drawdown by 3:30.
recipes.why: Two-pour structure for clarity and even extraction; forgiving on grind.
recipes.author: James Hoffmann

recipes.name: Aerocano (improved iced americano)
recipes.steps: Pull espresso over ice + water; steam/aerate briefly.
recipes.author: James Hoffmann

recipes.name: 4:6 Method
recipes.steps: First 40% in two pours controls sweetness/acidity; last 60% in 3 pours controls strength.
recipes.why: Tunable balance of sweetness vs. strength.
recipes.author: Tetsu Kasuya

recipes.name: Gong Fu with Hario Switch
recipes.steps: Multi-stage immersion + percolation hybrid for sweetness and clarity.
recipes.author: Lance Hedrick
